title: Day 1 Setup
description: Install Rust and get your editor ready in under 30 minutes.

steps.title: Install rustup
steps.description: Download and install the official Rust toolchain manager from rustup.rs.

steps.title: Verify installation
steps.description: Run `rustc --version` and `cargo --version` in your terminal.

steps.title: Set up your editor
steps.description: Install rust-analyzer for VS Code, Cursor, or Neovim for autocomplete and inline errors.

steps.title: Run your first program
steps.description: Create a project with `cargo new hello_rust` and run `cargo run`.

title: First Week Plan
description: A structured first week: read, practice, and join the community.

steps.title: Read chapters 1–4 of The Rust Book
steps.description: Covers installation, guessing game, common concepts, and ownership basics.

steps.title: Complete Rustlings exercises
steps.description: Hands-on drills that reinforce each chapter concept.

steps.title: Bookmark cheats.rs
steps.description: Keep the cheat sheet open while coding — you'll reference it constantly.

steps.title: Join the community
steps.description: Ask questions on users.rust-lang.org or the official Discord.

title: First CLI Tool
description: Build a real command-line program with Cargo — from project layout to parsing args and handling errors.

steps.title: Learn Cargo workflows
steps.description: Understand projects, dependencies, tests, and `cargo run` before writing your tool.

steps.title: Clone the Command-Line Rust repo
steps.description: Follow along with Ken Youens-Clark's project-based guide — each chapter builds a Unix-style tool.

steps.title: Build your first subcommand
steps.description: Start with a small `grep`- or `wc`-style binary using clap for argument parsing.

steps.title: Run Clippy before you ship
steps.description: Catch unidiomatic patterns early — essential habit for any CLI you share or open-source.

title: First Web API
description: Go from async basics to a production-ready HTTP service with Axum and real-world deployment patterns.

steps.title: Read the Async Book
steps.description: Grasp futures, async/await, and Tokio fundamentals before building an API.

steps.title: Explore the Axum framework
steps.description: Build routes, extractors, and JSON handlers — the modern Rust web stack.

steps.title: Follow the Tokio tutorial
steps.description: Practice spawning tasks and async I/O that Axum builds on.

steps.title: Study Zero to Production
steps.description: Learn testing, configuration, observability, and deployment for a service you'd run in prod.

title: Interview Week
description: A focused seven-day plan to sharpen fundamentals, practice problems, and tricky Rust semantics before interviews.

steps.title: Review ownership and lifetimes
steps.description: Re-read The Rust Book chapters 4–10 — the core of most Rust interview questions.

steps.title: Solve Exercism Rust exercises
steps.description: Practice writing idiomatic solutions with mentor feedback on each submission.

steps.title: Take the Rust Quiz
steps.description: Test yourself on surprising ownership, lifetime, and type-system edge cases.

steps.title: Work through interview Q&A
steps.description: 100 coding problems and 120 theoretical questions covering traits, async, and more.

steps.title: Try Rust Brain Teasers
steps.description: Short puzzles that expose gaps in your mental model — great last-minute prep.

steps.title: Follow the full Interview Prep path
steps.description: See all curated resources in order — including Crust of Rust and reading production code.

title: Contributing to OSS
description: Learn to read real Rust codebases, navigate tooling, and find your first meaningful open-source contribution.

steps.title: Set up rust-analyzer
steps.description: Go-to-definition, inline types, and diagnostics make reading unfamiliar code much faster.

steps.title: Clone and explore ripgrep
steps.description: A well-structured, widely admired codebase — start with `cargo build` and read `main.rs`.

steps.title: Watch Crust of Rust
steps.description: Jon Gjengset walks through real code — lifetimes, iterators, and patterns you'll see in the wild.

steps.title: Study Rust design patterns
steps.description: Recognize idioms in the code you read — builders, newtypes, RAII, and error handling.

steps.title: Pick a good first issue
steps.description: Filter rust-analyzer or ripgrep issues labeled `good first issue` or `E-easy` and ask on Discord if stuck.
